page: Microsoft Azure FX24-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 6 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42.0 GB per vCPU. It also includes 1417 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark testing, the server exhibits strong single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. Single-threaded text processing also ranks in the top tier. Multi-core performance, however, aligns with the middle 50% average, as seen in the stress-ng multi-core score and Geekbench multi-core score. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show an average read-only small block cached speed of 1035.31 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 59.39 GB/sec. For LLM workloads, the server achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 367.67 tokens/sec for a small 135M model, though larger models scale down to average performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX24-12mds_v2 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a specialized choice for workloads that require large datasets in memory without needing massive parallel CPU scaling. The tradeoff lies in its moderate physical core count, which limits highly parallelized multi-threaded compute efficiency compared to standard compute-optimized instances. This instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers such as Redis, and data analytics tasks that benefit from a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX24-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs, 504.0 GB of RAM, and 1417 GB of local storage. This configuration yields an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42.0 GB per vCPU. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores placing in the top 10%. Conversely, multi-core compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally align with mid-range averages. Lacking a GPU, this server is designed for memory-bound workloads rather than parallel graphics processing. It is highly suited for large in-memory databases, caching systems, and data-intensive enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-12mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density, fast single-core processing, and local SSD storage for data-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor.
504.0 GB of RAM providing a high 42.0 GB per vCPU memory density.
1417 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks.
Optimized for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
